title: The Moonlight Lake
paragraphs: Deep in a valley wrapped in mist, there was a lake that only glowed under the moonlight. The villagers called it Moonlight Lake.
Long ago, a fairy lived on the moon. Every night she would look down at the world below. One evening, she saw a little girl sitting by a stream, crying softly.
The girl's name was Yue. Her mother was very ill, and the village doctor said only a silver flower from the mountaintop could save her. But the mountain path was too dangerous for anyone to climb.
The moon fairy was moved by Yue's love for her mother. She gently sprinkled a beam of silver light from the moon. It fell into the stream, and the water began to glow.
Yue followed the glowing stream upward. The light guided her every step, and she was no longer afraid of the dark.
After a long, long walk, Yue finally reached the mountaintop. Silver flowers bloomed everywhere, shimmering under the moonlight like fallen stars.
Yue picked one flower and carefully carried it home. She brewed it into tea and gave it to her mother to drink.
The next morning, her mother was well again. And the little lake where the stream flowed began to glow with a soft silver light every full moon.
People say it is the moon fairy's blessing, watching over every kind child in the valley.
The night grows deeper now. Moonlight gently touches the surface of the lake, and everything becomes quiet. Goodnight.
